name: loop_slice_keep_quads
description: Loop Slice 'Keep Quads' option (task 0249). Custom planar mesh: two unit quads Q0=[0,1,4,3] and Q1=[1,2,5,4] side by side, capped on the far side by a triangle T=[2,6,5]. The seed edge is the vertical edge (1,0,0)-(1,1,0) shared by Q0 and Q1. The quad ring walks {Q0,Q1} and TERMINATES at the non-quad T (walkRingSide stops at the triangle). Three midpoints are inserted at 0.5 of the seed edge (1,0.5), Q0's left boundary edge (0,0.5) and Q1's exit rail (2,0.5) — so both ON and OFF land 10 verts / 5 faces. The DIFFERENCE is at the non-quad termination: with Keep Quads OFF (default, whole-ring behaviour byte-for-byte) T is re-emitted UNCUT, so the exit rail (2,1)-(2,-... i.e. edge v2-v5) carries a T-junction — the full edge (2,5) survives ALONGSIDE its two half-edges (2,mid),(mid,5) => 15 edges. With Keep Quads ON, T ABSORBS the terminating midpoint (2,0.5) into its boundary, becoming the quad [2,6,5,mid]; the full edge (2,5) is gone => 14 edges. Edge count (15 vs 14) is the countable proof that the non-quad absorbed the midpoint, turning the cut watertight + all-quad. Reference-independent geometry law: every new vertex sits at the 0.5 midpoint of a pre-op edge (identical vertex set both ways).
source: Analytic self-golden. The 'Keep Quads' rule is DERIVED from the live-captured reference `quad` attribute (toolcards/mesh.loopSlice spec.json, task 0244 live probe: 'Keep quad polygons as possible -- forces all new polys inside the sliced area to stay quadrangular', default off, control conditionally-enabled). The reference loop-slice gesture cannot be driven headlessly (needs a human VNC drive), so the ON/OFF termination topology is NOT independently re-captured live. vibe3d's quad ring already splits only quads (every NEW sub-face is a quad regardless), so the realized ON/OFF difference is the watertight all-quad TERMINATION at a non-quad boundary (absorb the midpoint vs leave the non-quad uncut / T-junction) — reusing the same absorb machinery as `select` (task 0248). Flagged honestly.
